documentation:
## Requirements
- PyTorch (1.0+)
- python 3

## Installation 
```shell
pip install arm-pytorch-utilities
```
You can also install in editable mode with `python3 -m pip install -e .` so that modifications
in the repository are automatically synced with the installed library

## Testing
Install `pytest` if you don't have it, then run
```
py.test
```

classes.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.softknn.SoftKNN

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.softknn.SoftKNN.__init__
documentation:
---
param min_k: Minimum number of neighbours to consider at each point; the behaviour
  depends on what activation
---

is used and whether a uniform threshold is chosen
\:param activation: The function to apply to the shifted distances to get positive weights; currently there is
'linear' which is ReLU and otherwise we use sigmoid. We recover hard KNN by using the sign activation function
(1 for distance >= 0 and 0 otherwise).
\:param uniform_threshold: Whether a single distance threshold should be chosen for all data points such that
at least min_k points is guaranteed to have positive weights for every data point
\:param normalization: None or the p value for the L_p norm, use 1 for sum to 1, use 2 for norm to 1

Differentiable relaxation of the k-nearest neighbour operation.
For a N x n feature input, we output a N x N weight matrix such that the (i,j) element is the weight of the
jth data point of being one of the k nearest neighbours of the ith data point. Weights are non-negative and
their upper value range depends on initialization options.

documentation:
Throws a ValueError if value.shape != expected_shape.

: "z23a" -> ["z", 23, "a"]

```

return

```

documentation:
In place sort list in the way that humans expects; from <https://nedbatchelder.com/blog/200712/human_sorting.html>
\:param l: list

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.tensor_utils.handle_batch_input
documentation:
Decorator for functions that expect n-dimensional input, handle input that have more than n dimensions by
flattening them temporarily and intelligently reshaping the output to match the input.
from arm_pytorch_utilities, standalone since that package is not on pypi yet

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.tensor_utils.ensure_diagonal
documentation:
Ensure that Q is a (dim x dim) diagonal matrix either with values of Q, or with eye * Q if Q is scalar
\:param Q: (dim x dim) tensor or numpy array, or a (dim) vector of the diagonal, or a scalar to scale an identity
matrix by
\:param dim: size of matrix
\:return: (dim x dim) diagonal matrix

modules.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.gmm
documentation: GMM from GPS.

classes.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.gmm.GMM

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.gmm.GMM.inference
documentation:
Evaluate dynamics prior.
Args:
    pts: A N x D array of points.

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.gmm.GMM.estep
documentation:
Compute log observation probabilities under GMM.
Args:
    data: A N x D array of points.
Returns:
    logobs: A N x K array of log probabilities (for each point
        on each cluster).

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.gmm.GMM.moments
documentation:
Compute the moments of the cluster mixture with logwts.
Args:
    logwts: A K x 1 array of log cluster probabilities.
Returns:
    mu: A (D,) mean vector.
    sigma: A D x D covariance matrix.

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.gmm.GMM.clusterwts
documentation:
Compute cluster weights for specified points under GMM.
Args:
    data: An N x D array of points
Returns:
    A K x 1 array of average cluster log probabilities.

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.gmm.GMM.update
documentation:
Run EM to update clusters.
Args:
    data: An N x D data matrix, where N = number of data points.
    K: Number of clusters to use.
Gaussian Mixture Model.

modules.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.optim
documentation:
from https://github.com/michaelrzhang/lookahead
usage

optimizer = # {any optimizer} e.g. torch.optim.Adam
if args.lookahead:
    optimizer = Lookahead(optimizer, la_steps=args.la_steps, la_alpha=args.la_alpha)

classes.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.optim.Lookahead

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.optim.Lookahead.__init__
documentation:
optimizer: inner optimizer
la_steps (int): number of lookahead steps
la_alpha (float): linear interpolation factor. 1.0 recovers the inner optimizer.
pullback_momentum (str): change to inner optimizer momentum on interpolation update

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.optim.Lookahead._backup_and_load_cache
documentation: Useful for performing evaluation on the slow weights (which typically generalize better)

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.optim.Lookahead.step
documentation:
Performs a single Lookahead optimization step.
Arguments:
    closure (callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model
        and returns the loss.

PyTorch implementation of the lookahead wrapper.
Lookahead Optimizer: https://arxiv.org/abs/1907.08610

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.linalg.batch_quadratic_product
documentation:
Batch multiplication of x^T A x.
\:param X: N x nx where each x is a row in X
\:param A: nx x nx
\:returns N x 1 product of each x^T A x

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.linalg.batch_outer_product
documentation:
Batch outer product uv^T
\:param u: N x nx
\:param v: N x nx
\:return: N x nx x nx where each 'row' is the outer product of each row of u and v

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.linalg.batch_batch_product
documentation:
Batch multiplication of y = Ax where both A and x are batched
\:param X: N x nx
\:param A: N x ny x nx
\:return: N x ny product of each A with each x

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.linalg.kronecker_product

Computes the Kronecker product between two tensors.
See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kronecker_product

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.linalg.cov

classes.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.linalg.MatrixSquareRoot

Square root of a positive definite matrix.
From https://github.com/steveli/pytorch-sqrtm
NOTE: matrix square root is not differentiable for matrices with
      zero eigenvalues.

Efficient implementation of gels from
Nanxin Chen
bobchennan@gmail.com

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.grad.jacobian
documentation:
Compute the jacobian of f evaluated at x. If f is a network or has parameters, freezing it
(set requires_grad = False) improves performance (especially on large networks)

From <https://gist.github.com/sbarratt/37356c46ad1350d4c30aefbd488a4faa>
\:param f: f(x) -> y
\:param x: 1 x nx or nx input to evaluate the jacobian at
\:return: df/dx(x) ny x nx jacobian of f evaluated at x

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.grad.batch_jacobian
documentation:
Compute jacobian for a batch of x
\:param f: f(x) -> y (supporting batch input B x \<some num> x nx), such as a NN
\:param x: B x nx
\:return: df/dx(x) B x ny x nx jacobian of f evaluated at each x

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.trajectory.estimate_cost
documentation: Returns cost derivatives and computed dynamics via a forward pass

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.trajectory.dlqr
documentation:
Solve the discrete time lqr controller.

x[k+1] = A x[k] + B u[k]

cost = sum x[k].T*Q*x[k] + u[k].T*R*u[k]

variables.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.trajectory.CHECK_FINITE
documentation: False is faster

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.make_data.datasource.DataSource.update_preprocessor
documentation:
Change the preprocessor,
which involves remaking the data and potentially changing the meaning/config of the data.
If you need the loaded untransformed config (for example for doing control in that space), then you should
not pass the preprocessor in the constructor, but instead call this function with it afterwards.

```
param preprocessor



return

original data configuration (before any transformation, even if this is not the first transform)
```
Source of data, agnostic to its creation method; public API for data

Angle between 2 n-dimensional vectors

:param u N x n tensor
:param v M x n tensor
:return N x M angle in radians between each tensor

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.math_utils.angle_between_stable
documentation:
Angle between 2 n-dimensional vectors. This is a numerically stable version (acos behaves poorly around 1 and -1)

:param u N x n tensor
:param v M x n tensor
:return N x M angle in radians between each tensor

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.math_utils.angle_between_batch
documentation:
Angle between 2 n-dimensional vectors. This is a numerically stable version (acos behaves poorly around 1 and -1)
See <https://math.stackexchange.com/questions/1143354/numerically-stable-method-for-angle-between-3d-vectors>

\:param u N x n tensor
\:param v N x n tensor
\:param dim: dimension to reduce
\:return N angle in radians between each tensor

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.math_utils.angular_diff_batch

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.math_utils.angle_normalize

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.math_utils.cos_sim_pairwise
documentation:
Pairwise cosine similarity between 2 sets of vectors

From <https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/11202>
\:param x1: (N, nx) N tensors of nx dimension each
\:param x2: (M, nx) M tensors of nx dimension each, equal to x1 if not specified
\:param eps: threshold above 0 to reduce dividing by 0
\:return: (N, M) pairwise cosine similarity

classes.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.model.mdn.MixtureDensityNetwork
documentation:
Mixture density network; implementation mostly from https://github.com/tonyduan/mdn.
Used as the final/output block of a network.
[ Bishop, 1994 ]
Parameters
----------
dim_in: int; dimensionality of the covariates
dim_out: int; dimensionality of the response variable
n_components: int; number of components in the mixture model

documentation: This file defines the linear Gaussian policy class from GPS.

classes.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.policy.lin_gauss.LinearGaussianPolicy

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.policy.lin_gauss.LinearGaussianPolicy.act
documentation:
Return an action for a state.
Args:
    x: State vector.
    obs: Observation vector.
    t: Time step.
    noise: Action noise. This will be scaled by the variance.

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.policy.lin_gauss.LinearGaussianPolicy.fold_k
documentation:
Fold noise into k.
Args:
    noise: A T x Du noise vector with mean 0 and variance 1.
Returns:
    k: A T x dU bias vector.

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.policy.lin_gauss.LinearGaussianPolicy.nans_like
documentation:
Returns:
    A new linear Gaussian policy object with the same dimensions
    but all values filled with NaNs.

Time-varying linear Gaussian policy.
U = K*x + k + noise, where noise ~ N(0, chol_pol_covar)

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.draw.clear_ax_content
documentation: Clear a matplotlib axis's content without changing its limits or labels

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.draw.confidence_ellipse
documentation:
Create a plot of the covariance confidence ellipse around center with covariance cov

Parameters
----------
ax : matplotlib.axes.Axes
    The axes object to draw the ellipse into.

n_std : float
    The number of standard deviations to determine the ellipse's radiuses.

Returns
-------
matplotlib.patches.Ellipse

Other parameters
----------------
kwargs : `~matplotlib.patches.Patch` properties

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.draw.highlight_value_ranges
documentation: Highlight the background of current figure with a label array; a value of 0 is left blank

functions.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.draw.cumulative_dist
documentation:
Plot the empirical CDF of some series

```
param series

Iterable of 1D tensors (or a 2D tensor with each row being a series)

param labels

Names for each of the series (iterable of strings)

param xlabel

Name for the value the series are over

param indicators

Indicator variables for each data point in each series to indicate a special event

return

figure
```

classes.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.load_data.DataConfig

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.load_data.DataConfig.__init__
documentation:
---
param expanded_input: Whether the input has extra dimensions (packed in control dimension)
param force_affine: Whether a column of 1s should be added to XU to make the dataset
  effectively affine
param predict_all_dims: Whether the prediction should include all state dimensions
  (some datasets do this regardless)
param predict_difference: Whether the prediction should be the state difference or
  the next state
param sort_data: Whether the experiments (data between files) are sorted (by random
  seed id)
param y_in_x_space: Whether the output is in the same space as state; if not then
  a lot of assumptions will not hold
---
Data class holding configuration information about a dataset

Turn a file's dictionary content into a data sequence, each element of which has the same number of rows

Driver for loading a dataset from file.
Each dataset should subclass DataLoader and specialize process file raw data to saved content.

Different from pytorch's transforms as those are typically applied as you retrieve the data (usually images)

methods.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.preprocess.SingleTransformer.data_dim_change
documentation:
How this transform changed the dimension of the data.

Also gives enough info to tell if it changed nx or nu

```
return

(change in dimensions, smallest index of dimension created/removed)
```
Transformer like Sklearn's transformers; pytorch version to pass through differentiation

classes.name: arm_pytorch_utilities.preprocess.DatasetPreprocessor
documentation:
Wrapper around Transformer for datasets
with a transformation fitted on the training set and applied to both training and validation set.
Assumes the dataset is from a dynamical system (X,U,Y,labels)
